sheets.name: Dashboard
sampleRows: How to use: add reusable foods/nicknames on the Foods tab, then log each meal on Meal Log. Daily Summary and Dashboard update automatically.

sheets.name: Foods
sampleRows: 1 shake: whole milk + protein powder + 1 cup blueberries
Adjust if you change milk amount/type.
Calorie dense; try 1/2 cup when cutting.
Estimate depends on cereal and milk volume.
Assumes whole wheat bread, about 2 oz turkey, and 2 slices cheddar or Swiss.
Very hydrating, low calorie, good source of vitamins A and C.
Excellent lean protein, rich in B vitamins.
High in fiber, vitamin C, vitamin K, and folate.
Good source of potassium and vitamin C; score assumes moderate oil when roasted.

sheets.name: Instructions
sampleRows: On Foods, define nicknames and calories once, e.g., Blueberry Protein Shake.
On Meal Log, enter Date, Meal, Food, and Quantity after each meal from your phone.
Use decimal quantities for partial servings, e.g., 0.5 for half a cup of mixed nuts/raisins.
If a food is missing, add it to Foods first; the Meal Log will then calculate automatically.
Update Weight Log whenever you weigh yourself.
Use Daily Summary and Dashboard to watch weekly calorie average, protein, nutrition score, and weight trend.
Formulas use common Google Sheets/Excel functions such as VLOOKUP, SUMIFS, COUNTIFS, FILTER, LOOKUP, and IFERROR.
For faster phone entry, create a Google Form later with Date, Meal, Food, Quantity fields connected to Meal Log.
Initial default targets: 2,000 calories/day, 100g protein/day, nutrition score 7+. Adjust based on your weight trend.
